core_mechanism: a decision-theoretic admissibility / complete-class argument: equipping a space of candidate procedures with a dominance partial order and identifying the minimal complete class of undominated (admissible) elements that collectively dominate every other procedure
brief_reason: The abstract defines strong/weak dominance relations, proves every procedure is dominated by an admissible weighted-mean member forming a complete class, and characterizes largest/undominated elements within a symmetric subclass — the classic admissibility and complete-class structure of statistical decision theory.
